_comment: openrabbit — organization ruleset (PRD §11 enforcement).
Requires the openrabbit review workflow/check to pass before a PR can merge
into the default branch of every targeted repo, governed CENTRALLY from the
org's .github repo — no per-repo branch-protection drift.
ROLLOUT (SPEC §11): create this ruleset in 'evaluate' enforcement first so it
reports would-block decisions WITHOUT blocking any merge; once the eval run
shows the openrabbit check is green and stable across the fleet, flip
'enforcement' to 'active' to make it a hard merge gate.
REQUIRED-CHECK CONTEXT (read carefully): for a reusable-workflow job,
GitHub reports the check-run name as '<caller job name> / <reusable job name>'.
Both onboarding paths name the caller job 'openrabbit' (the org starter
template sets `name: openrabbit`; the init-generated caller uses job id
'openrabbit' with no explicit name, which GitHub renders as 'openrabbit') and
the reusable workflow names its job 'review', so the EXACT reported context is
'openrabbit / review' — that is the string in required_status_checks below.
If you rename either job you MUST update this context (and safe-settings.yml).
SHA-PIN (SPEC §12): 'required_status_checks.integration_id' optionally pins
the check to GitHub Actions. The reusable workflow itself is SHA-pinned in each
repo's caller (see workflow-templates/openrabbit.yml, which uses
'<OWNER>/openrabbit/.github/workflows/reusable-workflow.yml@<PINNED_SHA>').
Replace <OWNER>/<PINNED_SHA> with your org and the vetted openrabbit release
SHA; a single central bump of that SHA rolls every onboarded repo forward.
INTEGRATION_ID 15368 = the GitHub Actions app. Pinning the required check to
this integration_id ensures ONLY GitHub Actions (the workflow above) can post
the 'openrabbit / review' status — a forged status from another app/PAT cannot
satisfy the gate. 15368 is GitHub Actions' app id on github.com; VERIFY it for
your GitHub host (GHES has its own id) before relying on it:
gh api /apps/github-actions --jq .id        # expect 15368 on github.com
If your host returns a different id (e.g. on GHES), replace 15368 below. To
drop the pin entirely (accept the check from any app), remove the
'integration_id' key. `gh openrabbit doctor` validates the OIDC trust policy;
this comment is how you validate the integration_id.
Create via: gh api -X POST /orgs/<ORG>/rulesets --input org/ruleset.json
name: openrabbit required review
